nodes.label: AI as better internet search
nodes.grounding_span: normally I use it as a better form of internet searching

nodes.label: AI for technical reference and brainstorming
nodes.grounding_span: figuring out the appropriate git commands to do things, deciding between SQL approaches, etc. I also use it to brainstorm wording

nodes.label: AI reliability for mission-critical accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: mission critical and needs to be 100% accurate like if a SQL version supports certain features

nodes.label: AI for internal infrastructure
nodes.label_negative: Internal search engines for proprietary knowledge
nodes.grounding_span: questions related to technical internal infrastructure or patterns are when I'll use our internal search engines and tools since AI wont know anything about that

nodes.label: AI for code writing
nodes.grounding_span: I don't use things like copilot or other AI tools to write code

nodes.label: AI for writing documentation and communication
nodes.label_negative: Manual writing to maintain skill and pride
nodes.grounding_span: another task I like to do myself is writing technical documentation or internal communication/emails

nodes.label: AI for creative tasks like presentations
nodes.grounding_span: creating a powerpoint presentation - I enjoy being a little creative with presentations

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: I think more of collaborating, like I'll ask for a SQL approach, and then follow up with modifications

nodes.label: AI honesty about limitations
nodes.label_negative: AI overpromising or not admitting inability
nodes.grounding_span: the AI refused to say it was impossible until I pressured it

nodes.label: AI for problem solving buffering
nodes.grounding_span: I'm wiling to use AI to buff my problem solving

nodes.label: AI for summarization and investigation
nodes.grounding_span: I do intend to use AI for more summarization and investigation

nodes.label: Efficiency and convenience
nodes.grounding_span: better form of internet searching

nodes.label: Accuracy and reliability
nodes.grounding_span: mission critical and needs to be 100% accurate

nodes.label: Personal enjoyment and identity
nodes.grounding_span: I enjoy the physical act of typing to write code

nodes.label: Skill preservation and pride
nodes.grounding_span: I pride myself a little bit on my writing skills

nodes.label: Independence and self-reliance
nodes.grounding_span: I really don't like having to rely on things or people other than myself

nodes.label: Preference for AI over web search for common queries
nodes.grounding_span: if its something that I would google/web search to find the answer, i will just use AI instead

nodes.label: Reluctance to use AI for mission-critical accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: mission critical and needs to be 100% accurate like if a SQL version supports certain features

nodes.label: Resistance to AI for code writing due to enjoyment
nodes.grounding_span: I don't use things like copilot or other AI tools to write code

nodes.label: Resistance to AI for writing to maintain skill
nodes.grounding_span: another task I like to do myself is writing technical documentation or internal communication/emails

nodes.label: Frustration with AI not admitting limitations
nodes.grounding_span: the AI refused to say it was impossible until I pressured it

nodes.label: Openness to using AI for summarization and investigation
nodes.grounding_span: I do intend to use AI for more summarization and investigation

nodes.label: Decision-tree based tool selection
nodes.grounding_span: if its something that I would google/web search to find the answer, i will just use AI instead

nodes.label: Skill preservation prioritization
nodes.grounding_span: I want to maintain some skills myself (coding, writing)
